label: Kabul
sub: Afghan capital and seat of Taliban's Islamic Emirate government since August 15, 2021. Previously held by Soviets, mujahideen factions, and the US-backed republic.

label: Kandahar
sub: Taliban spiritual heartland and second largest city. Supreme leader Haibatullah Akhundzada believed to reside here. Fell to Taliban August 12, 2021.

label: Bagram Airfield
sub: Primary US/NATO air base in Afghanistan for 20 years. Handed over to Afghan forces July 2, 2021; now controlled by Taliban. Located 40km north of Kabul.

label: Tora Bora
sub: Mountain cave complex in Nangarhar where bin Laden was cornered in Dec 2001 but escaped to Pakistan. Battle involved over 1,000 al-Qaeda fighters.

label: Helmand Province
sub: Most contested province of the war; historically produced 95% of Afghan opium. Scene of intense British and US fighting 2006–2014. Taliban stronghold throughout conflict.

label: Lashkar Gah
sub: Capital of Helmand province. Site of intense fighting between Afghan security forces and Taliban in 2021. Fell to Taliban in August 2021 after prolonged siege.

label: Kunduz
sub: First provincial capital to fall to Taliban in 2015 (recaptured) and again in 2021. Scene of MSF hospital bombing by US AC-130 in October 2015 killing 42.

label: Nangarhar (ISIS-K Heartland)
sub: Nangarhar province, particularly Achin district, is ISIS-K's primary stronghold in Afghanistan. US dropped MOAB here in April 2017. ISIS-K maintains cells despite Taliban pressure.

label: Jalalabad
sub: Capital of Nangarhar province. Key crossroads city near Pakistan border and scene of multiple ISIS-K attacks. Fell to Taliban without a fight in August 2021.

label: Islamabad
sub: Pakistani capital and seat of government managing the complex Af-Pak policy. Site of strategic talks and target of multiple TTP-inspired attack plots.

label: Peshawar
sub: Capital of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province and frequent target of TTP bombings. January 2023 mosque suicide bomb killed 100+ police officers.

label: Khyber Pass
sub: Historic mountain pass forming main land route between Pakistan and Afghanistan. Primary NATO supply corridor until Pakistan closed it 2011-2012. Now controlled by Pakistan military.

label: Torkham Border Crossing
sub: Main Af-Pak border crossing on Khyber Pass route. Frequently closed during Pakistan-Afghanistan tensions. Scene of armed confrontations between security forces of both nations.

label: Spin Boldak / Chaman
sub: Southern Af-Pak border crossing in Kandahar. Major smuggling route and Taliban logistics corridor throughout conflict. Scene of frequent border skirmishes since 2021.

label: Abbottabad (OBL Compound)
sub: Site of Osama bin Laden's compound where he was killed by US Navy SEAL Team 6 on May 2, 2011, in Operation Neptune Spear. Located near Pakistani Military Academy.

label: North Waziristan
sub: Former al-Qaeda and TTP stronghold; contained Haqqani Network's main base. Pakistani military operations in 2014 (Zarb-e-Azb) largely cleared militants but many fled to Afghanistan.

label: South Waziristan
sub: Original TTP heartland under Baitullah Mehsud. Scene of major Pakistani military operations 2009-2010. Mehsud killed by US drone strike in 2009.

label: Marjah
sub: Taliban-controlled town in Helmand cleared in Operation Moshtarak (Feb 2010) by 15,000 NATO/Afghan troops. 'Government in a box' promised but governance failed. Taliban later returned.

label: Panjwai District
sub: Site of Operation Medusa (Sep 2006) where 500+ Taliban were killed. Also site of Robert Bales' massacre of 16 civilians in March 2012. Taliban heartland throughout conflict.

label: Herat
sub: Western Afghanistan's major city with strong Iranian connections. Fell to Taliban August 12, 2021. Devastated by 6.3-magnitude earthquake October 7, 2023, killing 1,400+.

label: Mazar-e-Sharif
sub: Northern city and first to fall to Northern Alliance in November 2001. German-led Provincial Reconstruction Team HQ. Fell to Taliban August 14, 2021, ending last major resistance.

label: Camp Bastion / Leatherneck
sub: Massive joint UK-US base in Helmand province. UK forces' main operating base. Attacked by 15 Taliban fighters in September 2012, killing 2 Marines and destroying 8 aircraft.

label: Kandahar Airfield
sub: Second largest NATO base in Afghanistan, south of Kandahar city. Major Canadian, US and multinational hub for southern operations. Returned to Afghan forces then Taliban in 2021.

label: Camp Chapman (Khost)
sub: CIA base in Khost province. Site of Dec 30, 2009 suicide bombing by Jordanian double-agent killing 7 CIA officers — one of worst losses in CIA history.

label: Khost
sub: Border city in southeastern Afghanistan; Haqqani Network stronghold. Site of frequent cross-border attacks from Pakistan. CIA's main forward operating base for eastern operations.

label: Paktika (Pakistan Airstrikes)
sub: Afghan province bordering Pakistan that was targeted by Pakistani airstrikes in March 2024 targeting TTP bases, killing civilians and militants. Triggered Taliban artillery retaliation.

label: Ghazni
sub: Ancient city on Kabul-Kandahar highway briefly captured by Taliban in August 2018 in a major offensive. Recaptured by Afghan forces with US air support. Fell permanently in 2021.

label: Farah
sub: Capital of Farah province in western Afghanistan. Repeatedly threatened and briefly overrun by Taliban. Fell to Taliban August 6, 2021 in the first provincial capital to fall in that offensive.

label: Zaranj (First Capital to Fall, 2021)
sub: Capital of Nimroz province and first provincial capital to fall in the Taliban's final offensive, captured August 6, 2021. Sits on Iran-Pakistan-Afghanistan trade route junction.

label: Badakhshan
sub: Remote northeastern province bordering Tajikistan and China. Fell to Taliban in early August 2021. Contains Wakhan Corridor — Afghanistan's narrow land border with China.

label: Kunar Province (ISIS-K)
sub: Mountainous eastern province bordering Pakistan that has been an ISIS-K stronghold since 2015. Difficult terrain limits Taliban counterterrorism operations. Frequent cross-border militant movement.

label: Nuristan Province
sub: Remote forested province that was never fully under government control. Site of the 2009 Battle of Kamdesh where 8 US soldiers were killed. ISIS-K maintains presence in mountain areas.

label: Kabul Serena Hotel Attack
sub: Luxury hotel attacked by Taliban gunmen on Jan 14, 2008, killing 8 including a Norwegian diplomat. Attacked again by Taliban in 2014. Symbol of repeated attacks on Kabul's 'secure zone.'

label: Kabul Airport / Abbey Gate
sub: Site of ISIS-K suicide bombing Aug 26, 2021, killing 13 US troops and 170 Afghans during evacuation. Most deadly day for US forces since 2011. Hamid Karzai International Airport.

label: Indian Embassy Bombing Site (2008)
sub: Site of July 7, 2008 truck bomb killing 58, attributed to Haqqani Network with suspected ISI backing. Second attack on Indian Embassy occurred in October 2009. Underscored Pakistan-India rivalry in Afghanistan.

label: Kandahar Massacre Site (2012)
sub: Village of Balandi in Panjwai district where US Staff Sgt. Robert Bales murdered 16 sleeping Afghan civilians on March 11, 2012, severely damaging US-Afghan relations.

label: MSF Hospital (Bombed 2015)
sub: Médecins Sans Frontières trauma hospital in Kunduz struck by US AC-130 on Oct 3, 2015, killing 42 people including 14 MSF staff. MSF called it a war crime. Hospital was later destroyed.

label: Quetta (Taliban Shura)
sub: Capital of Pakistan's Baluchistan province and longtime sanctuary for Taliban leadership ('Quetta Shura'). Mullah Omar reportedly died in hospital here in 2013. Taliban leaders maintained homes and offices.

label: Miranshah (Haqqani HQ)
sub: Main town in North Waziristan and historic headquarters of the Haqqani Network. Cleared by Pakistan in Operation Zarb-e-Azb (2014) but Haqqani commanders continued operating from other bases.

label: Rawalpindi (Bhutto Assassination)
sub: Site where Benazir Bhutto was assassinated by TTP suicide bomber on December 27, 2007. Pakistan's military city adjacent to Islamabad. Also HQ of Pakistan Army (GHQ).

label: Lahore (TTP Attacks)
sub: Pakistan's cultural capital targeted by TTP-linked militants in multiple major attacks including the 2009 police training academy massacre (90 killed) and 2010 Data Darbar shrine bombing.

label: Bamiyan (Buddhas Destroyed)
sub: UNESCO World Heritage site where Taliban destroyed the world's largest standing Buddhas in March 2001. Now under Taliban control. Province is Hazara-majority; historically marginalized by Taliban.

label: Sar-e-Pul (Hazara Massacres)
sub: Northern province where Taliban and ISIS-K committed atrocities against Hazara civilians, including the August 2017 Mirza Olang massacre killing 36+ Hazara civilians by Taliban forces.

label: Logar Province (Haqqani Network)
sub: Province south of Kabul historically dominated by Haqqani Network operations. Site of multiple car bombings and complex attacks targeting Kabul. Mohammad Jan Haqqani district is namesake.

label: Khunjerab Pass (China-Pak Border)
sub: World's highest paved international border between China and Pakistan. China's CPEC transit point into Gwadar. ISIS-K targeted Chinese workers in Pakistan, raising Beijing's security concerns.

label: Islam Qala (Iran-Afghan Border)
sub: Main Afghanistan-Iran border crossing in Herat province. Major drug smuggling route. Iran-Taliban relations complex; Iran has pragmatically engaged Taliban while hosting Afghan refugees.

label: Da Afghanistan Bank (Central Bank)
sub: Afghan central bank whose $9.5 billion in foreign reserves were frozen by the US after Taliban takeover. Taliban has been unable to access most funds. Causes severe economic hardship.

label: Zawahiri Safehouse (Sherpur, Kabul)
sub: Upscale Sherpur neighborhood compound where al-Qaeda leader Ayman al-Zawahiri was killed by CIA Hellfire R9X missile on July 31, 2022. House belonged to Haqqani Network leader.

label: Achin District (MOAB Drop Site)
sub: Nangarhar district and ISIS-K stronghold targeted by the 'Mother of All Bombs' (GBU-43/B MOAB) on April 13, 2017 — largest non-nuclear US bomb ever used in combat.

label: Daikundi Province
sub: Remote central Afghan province that was relatively peaceful under republic. Hazara-majority; Taliban appointed non-Hazara governor after 2021 takeover, displacing local leadership.

label: Wardak Province
sub: Province west of Kabul that Taliban used as base for attacks on the capital. US expelled from FOB Shindand after Afghan government demanded withdrawal over alleged torture of civilians.

label: Panjshir Valley (Resistance)
sub: Only province not to fall to Taliban immediately in 2021. Ahmad Massoud and Amrullah Saleh mounted resistance but were defeated by September 2021. Province remains under tight Taliban control.

label: Salang Tunnel / Pass
sub: Critical mountain pass connecting Kabul to northern Afghanistan. Strategic chokepoint throughout conflict. Soviet-era tunnel (3.4km) is major supply route. Taliban controls it since 2021.

label: Zabul Province
sub: Predominantly Pashtun province historically among the most insecure in Afghanistan. Romanian forces served here as part of NATO. High Taliban presence throughout conflict.

label: Uruzgan (Dutch NATO Sector)
sub: Remote south-central province where Dutch NATO forces served 2006-2010. Hamid Karzai's home province. Birthplace of Taliban movement in 1994. Dutch withdrawal was politically contentious in Netherlands.

label: Tarin Kowt (Camp Holland)
sub: Capital of Uruzgan province and site of Dutch NATO Camp Holland. Small city that was defended by Hamid Karzai with CIA assistance in December 2001 in one of the first battles of the war.

label: Sangin District, Helmand
sub: Considered the most dangerous district in Helmand during British operations. British suffered over 100 dead here. Handed to US Marines who also suffered heavy losses. Fell to Taliban in 2017.

label: Shaerzad District (ISIS-K Mountain Base)
sub: Remote mountainous district in Nangarhar used by ISIS-K as mountain base and training area. Site of sustained US and Taliban military operations targeting ISIS-K since 2015.

label: New Kabul-Bamyan Road (NZ/US Aid)
sub: Road built with NZ and US reconstruction funds symbolizing development efforts. Example of infrastructure projects that SIGAR found often unsustainable without continued international support.

label: Kandahar City (2021 Siege)
sub: Site of intense urban fighting in August 2021 as Taliban surrounded and besieged the city. Afghan commandos fought for 12 days before US airstrikes paused but city fell Aug 12, 2021.

label: Balkh Province / Mazar Base
sub: Northern province and site of ISAF regional command north headquarters at Camp Marmal (German-led). Province fell to Taliban August 14, 2021, ending northern resistance.

label: Nimroz Drug Smuggling Routes
sub: Remote southwestern province used for drug trafficking between Afghanistan and Iran/Pakistan. Taliban derives significant revenue from taxing opium and heroin shipments through this corridor.

label: Gwadar (CPEC Port)
sub: Pakistani port city at terminus of China-Pakistan Economic Corridor. Attacked by TTP and Baloch separatists. China's $62 billion CPEC investment creates security pressure on Pakistan.

label: Doha Taliban Political Office
sub: Taliban's official political office in Doha, Qatar established 2013. Site of Doha Agreement signing Feb 29, 2020 and subsequent US-Taliban negotiations. Taliban's primary diplomatic representation.

label: FATA (Operation Zarb-e-Azb Zone)
sub: Former Federally Administered Tribal Areas subjected to Pakistan's major military operation Zarb-e-Azb (2014-2017) targeting TTP, al-Qaeda, Haqqani Network. Merged into KPK province in 2018.

label: Kunduz PRT Base
sub: German-led Provincial Reconstruction Team base in Kunduz. Commander Gen. George Klein ordered controversial 2009 airstrike on fuel tankers killing 142 civilians. German military's bloodiest incident.

label: Kajaki Dam, Helmand
sub: Major hydroelectric dam on the Helmand River providing electricity to southern Afghanistan. UK and US spent hundreds of millions on upgrades. Control passed between Taliban and Afghan forces; now Taliban controls.

label: Swat Valley (TTP Occupation)
sub: Beautiful mountain valley in KPK controlled by TTP under Fazlullah 2007-2009 until Pakistani Army Operation Rah-e-Rast expelled militants. Malala Yousafzai's home valley; she was shot here in 2012.

label: Chitral (Af-Pak Border ISIS-K)
sub: Remote Pakistani district bordering Nuristan and Kunar. ISIS-K has used forested border area to infiltrate Pakistan. Pakistan has built border fence here as part of Anti-TTP/ISIS-K strategy.

label: Pul-e-Charkhi Prison
sub: Afghanistan's largest prison east of Kabul. Under Taliban rule now holds former government officials, soldiers, and human rights defenders. Taliban released thousands of prisoners in 2021.

label: Tor Ghar / Dir Districts (TTP)
sub: Remote KPK districts bordering Afghanistan used by TTP for cross-border operations and sanctuaries. Pakistan military has conducted multiple operations here targeting TTP commanders.

label: Gardez (Paktia Province)
sub: Capital of Paktia province in southeastern Afghanistan. Hub of coalition operations in RC-East. Site of February 2010 night raid killing Afghan civilians including pregnant women, causing major outrage.

label: Spin Ghar (White Mountains)
sub: Mountain range straddling Afghan-Pakistani border containing Tora Bora caves. Major al-Qaeda and ISIS-K base area. Scene of continuing US over-the-horizon strike operations.

label: Herat-Iran Border Zone
sub: Western Afghan border zone with Iran experiencing drug smuggling, refugee flows, and occasional armed confrontations between Taliban border forces and Iranian guards over water rights (Helmand River Treaty).
